name: Christopher D. Manning
recent_papers: Humans and transformer LMs: Abstraction drives language learning
Our paper on hallucinating legal LLMs
Effective Approaches to Attention-based Neural Machine Translation
interest_paragraph: I am particularly drawn to Professor Manning's pioneering work in applying deep learning to Natural Language Processing, especially his contributions to areas like Neural Machine Translation and Self-supervised Model Pre-training. I am also fascinated by the group's practical impact, evident in the development of widely used tools such as Stanza and Stanford CoreNLP, and their recent research on topics like 'hallucinating legal LLMs' which addresses critical real-world challenges in AI.
